title: Terraform apply blocked by stale state lock
symptoms: terraform apply errors with 'Error acquiring the state lock'
LockID is present in DynamoDB after a failed CI job
Nobody else is currently running Terraform
root_cause: CI runner crashed or was cancelled mid-apply and never released the lock
Network partition between runner and the DynamoDB backend
Manual `terraform apply` run with Ctrl+C
diagnosis: Check who owns the lock in the error message (Who/When fields)
Inspect the DynamoDB lock table for the LockID entry
Verify no other pipeline is still executing
fix: Force-unlock after confirming no live apply:

```bash
terraform force-unlock -force <LOCK_ID>
```
If state is corrupted, restore from the most recent S3 version:

```bash
aws s3api list-object-versions --bucket tf-state --prefix prod/terraform.tfstate
aws s3api copy-object --copy-source tf-state/prod/terraform.tfstate?versionId=<id> \
  --bucket tf-state --key prod/terraform.tfstate
```
prevention: Run Terraform with `-lock-timeout=10m` to wait instead of failing fast
Use short-lived credentials scoped per pipeline run
Alert on state locks held longer than 15 minutes

title: Terraform plan shows endless drift on tags
symptoms: Every plan shows the same tag being added and removed
Drift report from Terraform Cloud never reaches zero
Resources were modified in the AWS console
root_cause: An out-of-band change applied tags from a different owner
A provider default_tags block conflicts with per-resource tags
Auto-tagging policy (Service Control Policy) injects tags
diagnosis: terraform plan -refresh-only to see the delta
Inspect the resource in the console to locate the extra tag
Search the provider block for default_tags
fix: Adopt the tags into code using `default_tags` in the provider:
Run `terraform apply -refresh-only` to sync state without changes
prevention: Disable console write access in production accounts
Enable drift detection in Terraform Cloud
Store an allow-list of auto-applied tags in a shared module

title: Terraform provider operations timing out during apply
symptoms: Terraform apply hangs during provider operations
Context deadline exceeded errors in Terraform output
Operations succeed on retry but fail consistently first time
Long-running resource creation causing timeouts
root_cause: Default provider timeout too short for resource creation
Network latency between Terraform and cloud provider
Cloud provider API experiencing slow response times
Resource creation taking longer than expected
diagnosis: Check Terraform logs for timeout errors
Review provider configuration for timeout settings
Monitor cloud provider API response times
Test network connectivity to cloud provider
fix: Increase timeout in provider configuration with timeout setting
Add timeouts block to specific resources for create, update, and delete operations
Optimize network connectivity or run Terraform closer to provider
Break large operations into smaller sequential applies
prevention: Set appropriate timeouts for long-running resources
Run Terraform in regions close to cloud provider
Use Terraform Cloud/Enterprise for better reliability
Monitor and alert on long-running operations

title: Terraform module version conflicts causing apply failures
symptoms: Module version constraint not satisfied error
Different modules requiring incompatible versions of shared dependency
terraform init fails to download required module versions
Apply succeeds in one environment but fails in another
root_cause: Module version constraints too restrictive
Dependency graph has conflicting version requirements
Module registry not accessible or version not found
Local module cache corrupted or outdated
diagnosis: Review module version constraints in configuration
Check terraform init output for dependency resolution errors
Examine .terraform/modules directory for cached versions
Test module version compatibility locally
fix: Relax version constraints to allow compatible versions
Update module references to use compatible versions
Clear local module cache: rm -rf .terraform
Use terraform get -update to force module refresh
prevention: Use semantic versioning for module releases
Document module version compatibility matrix
Implement dependency review in CI/CD pipeline
Use Terraform module registry for version management

title: Terraform unable to reach remote backend for state operations
symptoms: Terraform init fails with backend unreachable error
State refresh operations timeout
Authentication errors when accessing remote state
Intermittent connectivity issues to backend
root_cause: Network connectivity issues to remote backend
Backend authentication credentials expired or invalid
Backend service down or experiencing issues
Firewall or proxy blocking backend access

Verify backend authentication credentials
Check backend service status
Review firewall and proxy settings
fix: Fix network connectivity to remote backend
Update backend authentication credentials
Configure proxy settings in backend configuration if needed
Switch to alternative backend temporarily if needed
prevention: Use Terraform Cloud/Enterprise for managed backend
Implement backend health monitoring
Document backend configuration and troubleshooting steps
Use credential rotation with automatic updates

title: Terraform unable to import existing resources into state

Imported resource does not match expected configuration
Resource ID not found or invalid

root_cause: Resource ID format incorrect for import
Resource configuration does not match actual resource
Provider not configured with correct permissions
Resource already exists in state with different ID
diagnosis: Verify resource exists and is accessible
Check provider documentation for correct import ID format
Compare resource configuration with actual resource attributes
Review terraform import error message for details
fix: Use correct resource ID format for import
Update resource configuration to match actual resource
Ensure provider has necessary permissions
Remove resource from state if incorrectly imported: terraform state rm
prevention: Document import procedures for each resource type
Test import in non-production environment first
Use terraform plan to compare configuration before import
Consider using terraform import for initial migration only

title: Terraform configuration has cyclic dependencies preventing apply
symptoms: Terraform plan shows circular dependency error
Apply fails with dependency cycle detected
Resources cannot be created in any order
Configuration changes introduce dependency issues
root_cause: Resources reference each other creating a cycle
Implicit dependencies created through data sources
Module structure creating circular dependencies
Lifecycle configuration creating dependency issues
diagnosis: Review terraform graph output to visualize dependencies
Check resource references for circular patterns
Examine module structure for dependency cycles
Use terraform plan -verbose to see dependency graph
fix: Break dependency cycle by removing unnecessary references
Use depends_on explicitly to control dependency order
Refactor module structure to eliminate cycles
Use null_resource or local values to break indirect dependencies
prevention: Design infrastructure with clear dependency hierarchy
Review dependency graph during code review
Use terraform graph in CI to detect cycles early
Document intended dependency relationships

Secrets passed as plain text variables

Check state file for unencrypted secrets
Examine CI/CD logs for exposed data
Review resource documentation for sensitive attributes
fix: Mark sensitive attributes with sensitive = true in output blocks
Enable state encryption at rest
Use environment variables or secret stores for sensitive inputs
Configure Terraform to hide sensitive values in plan output
prevention: Always use secret management for sensitive data
Enable state encryption for all backends
Review Terraform configuration for sensitive data exposure
Implement secret scanning in CI/CD pipeline

title: Terraform count and foreach conflicts causing resource duplication

Verify count value does not include null elements
Examine index references in resource dependencies
fix: Choose either count or foreach, not both
Use compact() function to remove null elements from count
Use foreach for complex iteration requirements
Fix index references to use correct syntax
prevention: Follow Terraform best practices for count vs foreach
Test configuration changes in terraform plan
Use locals to preprocess data before count/foreach
Document iteration strategy in module documentation

Document workspace strategy and naming conventions
